suggestions.title: Repair Lexi’s broken evidence-log and gdrive ingest commands
suggestions.description: This USB was just refreshed on June 7-8 to promise evidence logging and Google Drive ingest, but the packaged paths are broken right now: `Shared/generate-evidence-log.js` requires a missing `../web-app/...` module and the Python orchestrator points at a missing `../scripts/ingest_gdrive_evidence.py`. Codex can patch the sanitized package so the next evidence dump actually runs end to end.
suggestions.prompt: Fix the broken evidence workflow in this Lexi USB package. Audit the evidence-log and Google Drive ingest code paths, repair the bad module/script paths in the sanitized client, and make the packaged `/api/lexi/evidence-log` and `/api/lexi/ingest-gdrive` flows work locally from this project without depending on missing repo files.

suggestions.title: Load yesterday’s Waka ISRC catalog into Lexi for offline recall
suggestions.description: A new Google Drive spreadsheet, `iSRC Codes - QZ-9EM-17 (1).xlsx`, was viewed on June 7, and this project’s whole purpose is making Lexi useful for Waka’s work offline. Codex can turn that sheet into a local lookup/index so Lexi can answer catalog and code questions without reopening the spreadsheet every time.
suggestions.prompt: Add an offline catalog memory feature to this Lexi USB project using the latest Waka ISRC spreadsheet from Google Drive. Build a local importer or lookup layer so Lexi can answer track and ISRC questions from that sheet inside the USB app, and store the data in a format that survives across chats.

suggestions.title: Turn Moneypenny’s Drive dump into durable Lexi memory on this USB
suggestions.description: The local chat history shows repeated requests to make Lexi retain what was in the Moneypenny share folder and to absorb the `Stuck on the Sofa` canon, but the client still only has a plain text `projectMemory` field. Codex can build a real memory bootstrap so Lexi starts each session with the right Waka-specific context instead of being re-taught manually.
suggestions.prompt: Upgrade this Lexi client so it can keep durable Waka-specific memory from the Moneypenny Drive materials and the `Stuck on the Sofa` canon. Design a structured memory/bootstrap system in this project that persists on the USB, loads at startup, and is better than the current single `projectMemory` text blob.
